title: Coleus argentatus
taxonomy: Coleus argentatus is classified within the plant family Lamiaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mint family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its leaf shape, growth habit, and floral structure. The genus Coleus is further classified within the tribe Ocimeae, which comprises a diverse group of plants. Within the species Coleus argentatus, there may be different cultivars or varieties, each with unique characteristics. Further classification may depend on the specific cultivar or variety in question.

intro: Coleus argentatus, also known as Coleus aromaticus or Coleus, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The leaves and stems of Coleus argentatus have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, offering relief from various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coleus argentatus include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Coleus argentatus, such as infusions and decoctions, are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although modern studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
